message.content: {"items":[{"title":"Eurobarometer 88.3 (2017)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13282","justification":"Includes modules on European identity, cultural identity, globalisation and international politics; Netherlands is part of the sample; fieldwork in 2017 (within 2010s). Relevant for cultural/national identity, minorities’ attitudes and international organisations."},{"title":"Immigrant Citizens Survey (ICS), 2011: Belgium, France, Germany, Hungary, Italy, Portugal and the Netherlands","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.11647","justification":"Focuses on integration, political participation and subjective belonging of immigrants, including their sense of identity and experiences as minorities; conducted in 2011 and includes the Netherlands; closely linked to migration and minority identity in the 2010s."},{"title":"European Social Survey (ESS) Round 5 (2010)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10888","justification":"Cross‑national survey including the Netherlands; standard ESS core has questions on national attachment and identity as well as immigration and minorities; conducted in 2010, fitting the 2010s requirement."},{"title":"European Social Survey (ESS) Round 7 (2014)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12253","justification":"Includes the Netherlands; core questionnaire covers national identity and attitudes to immigrants; Round 7 also features special modules on immigration and aspects of political engagement, relevant to migration and minorities during the 2010s."},{"title":"European Social Survey (ESS) Round 8 (2016)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13316","justification":"Netherlands is part of the ESS sample; contains items on national and European identity and immigration; 2016 fieldwork places it squarely in the 2010s and it can be used to study cultural and national identity related to migration."},{"title":"European Social Survey (ESS) Round 9 (2018)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13314","justification":"Includes Dutch respondents and questions on national belonging, minorities and migration; timing (2018) allows analysis of identity and migration issues in the later 2010s."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme: National Identity III – ISSP 2013","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.11725","justification":"The 2013 ISSP National Identity module focuses explicitly on national and cultural identity, minorities and attitudes to immigration and international issues; Netherlands participates, and the 2013 fieldwork is in the 2010s."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme: National Identity IV – ISSP 2020 (pre-release, fieldwork beginning late 2019 in some countries)","url_or_doi":"https://dbk.gesis.org/dbksearch/sdesc2.asp?no=7390&db=e&doi=10.4232/1.13954","justification":"Module again on national identity, immigration and minorities, with items on international organisations; some participating countries fielded in late 2019. Where Dutch data are available starting 2019, it informs late‑2010s identity and migration debates."},{"title":"Eurobarometer 92.2 (2019)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13389","justification":"Standard Eurobarometer including the Netherlands; includes batteries on European and national identity, migration, and trust in international and EU institutions; carried out in 2019, capturing attitudes in the 2010s."},{"title":"Children of Immigrants Longitudinal Survey in Four European Countries (CILS4EU), 2010–2013, Netherlands component","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12368","justification":"Longitudinal survey among children of immigrants and comparison groups in several countries including the Netherlands; focuses on integration, ethnic and national identity, and minority status during 2010–2013, directly tied to migration and minority identity in the Netherlands."}]}
